description_long: This object is a classic cast iron kettlebell with a rounded, bulbous body and a thick, arched handle seamlessly integrated into the top. The surface is heavily textured, showing a mottled pattern of reddish-brown rust and darker iron, indicating significant weathering and exposure to the elements. The handle is smooth and rounded, designed for a secure grip, and is proportionally thick compared to the body. There are no visible brand logos or weight markings on any side, and the entire object appears to be a single solid piece of metal. The end caps are simply the rounded bottom of the kettlebell, with no additional features or patterns. The overall condition suggests it has been left outdoors or in a damp environment, as evidenced by the extensive rust and surface pitting.
description: A heavily rusted, solid cast iron kettlebell with a rounded body and thick, arched handle. The surface is mottled with reddish-brown rust and shows signs of significant wear.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full rounded body and the thick, arched handle, with a mottled rust texture dominating the surface.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the thickness of the handle and the seamless transition into the body, with rust and pitting visible along the side.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front, showing the rounded body and handle, with more pronounced dark patches of rust and weathering.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again emphasizes the handle's thickness and the uneven, weathered surface texture.
attribute_handle_type: Thick, arched, round cross-section, smooth and uncoated, no ergonomic features.
attribute_end_cap_design: Rounded, continuous with the body, no separate end caps, rough and pitted surface.
attribute_condition: Heavily rusted and weathered, with visible pitting and surface wear, no major structural damage.
